---
name: Feature request
about: Propose a new capability, surface, or workflow that the repo or the production system does not have today.
title: "[feature] "
labels: ""
assignees: ""
---

<!--
  Use this template when you want the repo or the operating surface to do
  something it does not do today. A feature request is the start of a
  conversation, not a commitment to ship — the operator decides what
  graduates into a PRD and an execution branch.
-->

## Problem

<!--
  The pain or gap. What can you not do today that you want to do? Who
  feels the pain — the operator, a client, a future operator, an auditor?
  Be concrete about the scenario that triggered the request.
-->

## Proposed solution

<!--
  Your best current idea for how to solve the problem. One paragraph or
  a short bulleted shape — not a full design. The full design lives in
  a PRD if and when this feature graduates to active execution.
-->

## Alternatives considered

<!--
  Other ways to solve the same problem and why they are worse. The point
  is not to relitigate — it is to make the decision space legible so the
  operator does not waste time re-deriving alternatives during triage.
-->

## Acceptance criteria

<!--
  How will we know this is done? Concrete, observable conditions — a
  command produces the expected output, a runbook step succeeds, a
  service exposes the expected endpoint. No "the system feels better"
  vibes-based criteria.
-->

- [ ]
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Constraints / scope

<!--
  Anything load-bearing that bounds the solution: vendor licensing
  (e.g., the n8n Sustainable Use License — see ADR 0003), security
  posture (e.g., no public TCP listeners — see ADR 0005), the build-
  in-public commit attribution (see ADR 0004). If the feature would
  conflict with a current ADR, name the conflict so triage can decide
  whether to revisit the ADR.
-->

## Related

<!--
  Links to related issues, PRDs, ADRs, runbooks, or chat artifacts.
  Empty is fine; if it is not empty, every link should be load-bearing.
-->
